title: Filipino Beef Stir Fry
description: A classic Filipino dish made with tender beef, stir-fried with onions, bell peppers, and a hint of soy sauce. This recipe is quick and easy to make, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the beef to the skillet and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the skillet.
Add the sliced onions and bell peppers to the skillet and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked beef back into the skillet and stir in the soy sauce.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot over steamed rice or noodles.
Garnish with chopped green onions and a sprinkle of toasted coconut flakes, if desired.
